criteria_verification.evidence: Implemented async def create_pr(worktree, title, body, base='main', draft=False) -> PRResult in src/forge/adapters/gh/operations.py. PRResult import is from forge.adapters.git.models per the shared adapter return contract (API-subprocess.md §4). TestCreatePrReturnContract has four asserts confirming PRResult is returned on missing-credentials, success, non-zero-exit, and exception paths.
criteria_verification.notes: Implemented async def create_pr(worktree, title, body, base='main', draft=False) -> PRResult in src/forge/adapters/gh/operations.py. PRResult import is from forge.adapters.git.models per the shared adapter return contract (API-subprocess.md §4). TestCreatePrReturnContract has four asserts confirming PRResult is returned on missing-credentials, success, non-zero-exit, and exception paths.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Pre-flight check: if `GH_TOKEN` is unset (or empty) in `os.environ`,
criteria_verification.evidence: create_pr reads os.environ.get('GH_TOKEN', '') on every call and returns PRResult(status='failed', error_code='missing_credentials', stderr='GH_TOKEN not set in environment') before reaching _execute. Tests test_missing_gh_token_returns_missing_credentials_failure and test_empty_gh_token_is_treated_as_missing both call execute_mock.assert_not_called() to prove gh is never invoked. test_gh_token_is_re_read_on_every_call verifies the token is not cached at import time.
criteria_verification.notes: create_pr reads os.environ.get('GH_TOKEN', '') on every call and returns PRResult(status='failed', error_code='missing_credentials', stderr='GH_TOKEN not set in environment') before reaching _execute. Tests test_missing_gh_token_returns_missing_credentials_failure and test_empty_gh_token_is_treated_as_missing both call execute_mock.assert_not_called() to prove gh is never invoked. test_gh_token_is_re_read_on_every_call verifies the token is not cached at import time.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: On success, parse the PR URL from gh's stdout and populate
criteria_verification.evidence: _build_command() assembles ['gh', 'pr', 'create', '--title', title, '--body', body, '--base', base] and appends '--draft' when draft=True. _execute is invoked with command=command, cwd=str(worktree). Tests test_default_command_shape_uses_separate_tokens and test_draft_flag_appends_dash_dash_draft assert the exact argv shape, including --base override and the cwd value /var/forge/builds/B1.
criteria_verification.notes: _build_command() assembles ['gh', 'pr', 'create', '--title', title, '--body', body, '--base', base] and appends '--draft' when draft=True. _execute is invoked with command=command, cwd=str(worktree). Tests test_default_command_shape_uses_separate_tokens and test_draft_flag_appends_dash_dash_draft assert the exact argv shape, including --base override and the cwd value /var/forge/builds/B1.

criteria_verification.evidence: _PR_URL_PATTERN = re.compile(r'https://github\.com/[^/]+/[^/]+/pull/(\d+)'). On exit_code==0, _PR_URL_PATTERN.search(stdout.strip()) extracts both the full URL (group 0) and the integer PR number (group 1). Tests test_success_parses_pr_url_and_number_from_stdout and test_pr_number_parsed_from_trailing_slash_component verify owner/repo/pull/123 and pull/4567 are parsed correctly with multiline stdout.
criteria_verification.notes: _PR_URL_PATTERN = re.compile(r'https://github\.com/[^/]+/[^/]+/pull/(\d+)'). On exit_code==0, _PR_URL_PATTERN.search(stdout.strip()) extracts both the full URL (group 0) and the integer PR number (group 1). Tests test_success_parses_pr_url_and_number_from_stdout and test_pr_number_parsed_from_trailing_slash_component verify owner/repo/pull/123 and pull/4567 are parsed correctly with multiline stdout.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Function body wrapped in `try/except Exception as exc:` returning a
criteria_verification.evidence: After _execute returns, if exit_code != 0 the function returns PRResult(status='failed', stderr=stderr.strip() or stdout.strip() or None). Test test_non_zero_exit_returns_failed_with_stderr asserts that exit_code=1 with stderr 'fatal: not a git repository' yields status='failed' with stderr preserved and pr_url/pr_number=None.
criteria_verification.notes: After _execute returns, if exit_code != 0 the function returns PRResult(status='failed', stderr=stderr.strip() or stdout.strip() or None). Test test_non_zero_exit_returns_failed_with_stderr asserts that exit_code=1 with stderr 'fatal: not a git repository' yields status='failed' with stderr preserved and pr_url/pr_number=None.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Arguments containing shell metacharacters (e.g. backticks in PR body)
criteria_verification.evidence: _execute is wrapped in try/except Exception as exc returning PRResult(status='failed', stderr=f'{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}'). Test test_execute_exception_is_caught_and_returned_as_failed uses AsyncMock(side_effect=RuntimeError('transport boom')) and asserts the call returns rather than raises, with stderr containing both the exception type and message. test_return_type_is_prresult_on_exception double-checks isinstance.
criteria_verification.notes: _execute is wrapped in try/except Exception as exc returning PRResult(status='failed', stderr=f'{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}'). Test test_execute_exception_is_caught_and_returned_as_failed uses AsyncMock(side_effect=RuntimeError('transport boom')) and asserts the call returns rather than raises, with stderr containing both the exception type and message. test_return_type_is_prresult_on_exception double-checks isinstance.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Unit tests: missing GH_TOKEN, success path with parsed URL, non-zero
criteria_verification.evidence: _build_command places title and body as their own list elements, never interpolated into a shell string. _execute uses asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(*command, ...) which spawns the binary directly without a shell. Tests test_backticks_in_body_pass_through_literal, test_dollar_signs_in_body_pass_through_literal, and test_metacharacters_in_title_pass_through_literal assert that bodies/titles like 'Run `rm -rf /` to test', 'Set $HOME and $PATH explicitly', and 'Fix `bug` in $service && rm tmp' appear verbatim as a single list token preceded by --body or --title.
criteria_verification.notes: _build_command places title and body as their own list elements, never interpolated into a shell string. _execute uses asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(*command, ...) which spawns the binary directly without a shell. Tests test_backticks_in_body_pass_through_literal, test_dollar_signs_in_body_pass_through_literal, and test_metacharacters_in_title_pass_through_literal assert that bodies/titles like 'Run `rm -rf /` to test', 'Set $HOME and $PATH explicitly', and 'Fix `bug` in $service && rm tmp' appear verbatim as a single list token preceded by --body or --title.
